content.query:
drop TABLE opt.claims_t;
create TABLE opt.claims_t 
WITH
(
	DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN,
	CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX
) AS
with adj_age as
(
  select 
    token, count(distinct patient_age) as cnt_ages, min(patient_age) as min_age, max(patient_age) as max_age, max(patient_age) - min(patient_age) as diff_age
  from 
    (
      select 
        token, case when patient_age >= 85 then 85 when patient_age <= 1 then 1 else patient_age end AS patient_age
      from 
        customer.stg_claims_t
    ) a
  group by token
)
select 
  c.token, 
  c.claim_num, 
  c.dos_begin, 
  c.dos_end, 
  -- redact gender
  coalesce(c.patient_gender, 'U') AS patient_gender,
  -- redact age
  coalesce(a.min_age, case when c.patient_age >= 85 then 85 when c.patient_age <= 1 then 1 else c.patient_age end) AS patient_age,
  -- redact zip3
  case when z2.zip3 is null then '000' else coalesce(z1.zip3_to, c.patient_zip_3) end AS patient_zip_3
from 
  customer.stg_claims_t c 
  left join adj_age a
    on c.token = a.token and a.cnt_ages = 2 and a.diff_age = 1
  left join dim.zip3_pop_lt_20k_t z1
    on c.patient_zip_3 = z1.zip3_from
  left join (select distinct zip3 from dim.census_population_by_zip3_age_gender_t) z2
    on coalesce(z1.zip3_to, c.patient_zip_3) = z2.zip3
;

-- select substr(dos_end, 1, 4), count(distinct input_token)
--   from customer.stg_claims_t
-- group by substr(dos_end, 1, 4)
-- order by 1;

-- select case when c.patient_zip3 = '000' or c.patient_zip3 is null then 'No Zip' when z2.zip3 is null then 'bad' else 'good' end as zip_validation, count(*)
-- from 
--   customer.stg_claims_t c 
--   left join (select distinct zip3 from dim.census_population_by_zip3_age_gender_v) z2
--     on c.patient_zip3 = z2.zip3
-- group by 1
-- ;
select *
  from customer.stg_claims_t
where PATIENT_ZIP_3 = ''
order by dos_begin;

-- select cnt_age, count(*)
-- from
-- (
--   select input_token, count(distinct patient_age) as cnt_age
--   from opt.claims_t
--   group by input_token
--   -- order by cnt_age desc
--   -- limit 50
-- )
-- group by cnt_age
-- limit 5

-- select * 
--   from opt.claims_t
-- where input_token = 'E9E707A498350E8BD2E34AA453DA91E1B54D2E515F5BFF4A8DE915CED611A8D053689CFEC357935A52924546C9C4CBB5'

-- select patient_age, count(distinct input_token) as cnt_patients
--   from opt.claims_t
-- group by 1
-- order by 1

-- Add generalization levels to the claim data
create table opt.claims_w_generalization_t 
WITH
(
	DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN,
	CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX
) as 
select distinct 
  a.input_token, 
  a.patient_age, 
  b.age_lvl1, 
  b.age_lvl2, 
  patient_gender, 
  patient_zip3,
  coalesce(c.msa, 'XX') as patient_msa 
  -- , coalesce(d.most_frequent_state, 'XX') as patient_state
FROM 
  opt.claims_t a
  inner join dim.age_ranges_t b
    on a.patient_age between b.age_lower_bound and b.age_upper_bound
  left join dim.zip3_msa_mapping_t c
    on a.patient_zip3 = c.ZIP3
-- left join dim.zip3_state_v d
--   on a.patient_zip3 = d.ZIP3
;

select cnt_rows, count(*)
from (
select input_token, count(*) as cnt_rows from opt.claims_w_generalization_t
group by input_token) a
group by cnt_rows
order by 1;

select count(distinct input_token) as cnt_tokens from opt.claims_w_generalization_t;

-- Add generalization levels to the census data
drop table dim.census_population_by_zip3_age_gender_distributed_t;
create table dim.census_population_by_zip3_age_gender_distributed_t
WITH
(
	DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN,
	CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX
) as
WITH numbers as (
  SELECT TOP 200  -- Adjust this number as needed
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY (SELECT NULL)) - 1 as number
  FROM sys.objects a 
)
select 
  b.number as age, a.zip3, a.gender, a.age_lower_bound, a.age_upper_bound, a.population / 5 as population
from 
  (
    select 
      a.zip3, a.gender, a.age_lower_bound, a.age_upper_bound, a.population
    from 
      dim.census_population_by_zip3_age_gender_t a
    where a.age_lower_bound < 85
  ) a
  inner join numbers b
   on b.number between a.age_lower_bound and a.age_upper_bound
union all
select 
  85 as age, a.zip3, a.gender, age_lower_bound, a.age_upper_bound, sum(a.population) as population
from 
  dim.census_population_by_zip3_age_gender_t a
where 
  a.age_lower_bound >= 85
group by 
  a.zip3, a.gender, age_lower_bound, a.age_upper_bound
;


select *
from dim.census_population_by_zip_age_gender_t
where substr(Geographic_Area_Name, 7, 3) in ('772', '205')


create table opt.claims_ref_population_t 
WITH
(
	DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN,
	CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX
) as
with base as
(
  SELECT 
    a.zip3, coalesce(c.msa, 'XX') as msa, gender
    , a.age, b.age_lower_bound, b.age_upper_bound, b.age_lvl1, b.age_lvl2
    , a.population as population
  FROM 
    dim.census_population_by_zip3_age_gender_distributed_t a
    inner join dim.age_ranges_t b
      on a.age between b.age_lower_bound and b.age_upper_bound
    left join dim.zip3_msa_mapping_t c
      on a.zip3 = c.ZIP3
  union all
  SELECT 
    a.patient_zip3 as zip3, a.patient_msa as msa, a.patient_gender as gender
    , a.patient_age as age, b.age_lower_bound, b.age_upper_bound, b.age_lvl1, b.age_lvl2
    , a.population as population
  FROM 
    opt.claims_population_not_in_census_v a
    inner join dim.age_ranges_t b
      on a.patient_age between b.age_lower_bound and b.age_upper_bound
)
select 
  zip3, msa, gender, age, age_lower_bound, age_upper_bound, age_lvl2, age_lvl1, population, 1 as data_grp
from base
union all
SELECT 
  a.zip3, a.msa, 'U' as gender, a.age, a.age_lower_bound, a.age_upper_bound, a.age_lvl2, a.age_lvl1, sum(a.population) as population, 2 as data_grp
FROM base a
where a.zip3 <> '000'
group by 
  a.zip3, a.msa, a.age, a.age_lower_bound, a.age_upper_bound, a.age_lvl2, a.age_lvl1
union all
SELECT 
  '000' as zip3, 'XX' as mas, a.gender, a.age, a.age_lower_bound, a.age_upper_bound, a.age_lvl2, a.age_lvl1, sum(a.population) as population, 3 as data_grp
FROM base a
where gender <> 'U'
group by 
  a.gender, a.age, a.age_lower_bound, a.age_upper_bound, a.age_lvl2, a.age_lvl1
union all
SELECT 
  '000' as zip3, 'XX' as msa, 'U' as gender, a.age, a.age_lower_bound, a.age_upper_bound, a.age_lvl2, a.age_lvl1, sum(a.population) as population, 4 as data_grp
FROM base a
group by 
  a.age, a.age_lower_bound, a.age_upper_bound, a.age_lvl2, a.age_lvl1
;


-- claim dimension table for generalization levels with corresponding losses
create table opt.claims_feature_generalization_t 
WITH
(
	DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN,
	CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX
) as
select 'gender' as feature, null as column_name, null as ref_column_name, 0 as level, null as mask, null as generalization_in_src
union all
select 'gender' as feature, 'patient_gender' as column_name, 'gender' as ref_column_name, 1 as level, 'U' as mask, null as generalization_in_src
union all
select 'zip' as feature, null as column_name, null as ref_column_name, 0 as level, null as mask, null as generalization_in_src
union all
select 'zip' as feature, 'patient_msa' as column_name, 'msa' as ref_column_name, 1 as level
 , null as mask, null as generalization_in_src
union all
select 'zip' as feature, 'patient_zip3' as column_name, 'zip3' as ref_column_name, 2 as level, '000' as mask, null as generalization_in_src
-- loss and generalization for age
union all
select 'age' as feature, null as column_name, null as ref_column_name, 0 as level, null as mask, null as generalization_in_src
union all
select 'age' as feature, 'age_lvl1' as column_name, 'age_lvl1' as ref_column_name, 1 as level, null as mask, null as generalization_in_src
union all
select 'age' as feature, 'age_lvl2' as column_name, 'age_lvl2' as ref_column_name, 2 as level, null as mask, null as generalization_in_src
union all
select 'age' as feature, 'patient_age' as column_name, 'age' as ref_column_name, 3 as level, null as mask, null as generalization_in_src
order by 1, 3
;


-- claim dimension table for feature weights
select *
from opt.claims_feature_weight_t;




with risk_tab as
(
  SELECT data.input_token, data.age, data.gender, data.zip3, data.cnt_patients, (ref.population) as population,
    case when ifnull((ref.population), 0) < 3 and data.cnt_patients < 3 then 'Yes'
      when ifnull((ref.population), 0) < 3 then 'Yes-Ref' 
    else 'No' end as high_risk
  FROM 
    (     
      SELECT 
        c.input_token, c.patient_age as age, c.patient_gender as gender, c.patient_zip3 as zip3, c.age_lvl1, c.age_lvl2,
        count(distinct c.input_token) over (partition by c.patient_age, c.patient_gender, c.patient_zip3) cnt_patients
      FROM opt.claims_w_generalization_t c
    ) AS data 
    left JOIN 
    (     
      SELECT age, gender, zip3 as zip3, SUM(population) / COUNT(DISTINCT data_grp) AS population
      FROM opt.claims_ref_population_t
      GROUP BY age, gender, zip3
    ) AS ref
      ON data.age = ref.age and data.gender = ref.gender and data.zip3 = ref.zip3
  where 
    ifnull((ref.population), 0) < 3 and data.cnt_patients >= 3
),
tab as
(
  select high_risk, count(distinct input_token) as cnt_patients from risk_tab
  group by 1
),
total as
(
  select count(distinct input_token) as cnt_patients from risk_tab
)
select a.*, a.cnt_patients / b.cnt_patients as risk_percentage
from tab a
cross join total b
order by 1
;
